mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a strengthening labor market, which drives higher aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-GFC labor market recovery ongoing since 2010.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in PAYEMS has a rolling 36-month rank correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns of less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a potential cyclical downturn, increasing recession risk and causing a flight to safety away from equities.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where the trigger condition is met over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is accelerating while the policy rate (FEDFUNDS) remains low, it signals the central bank is 'behind the curve', forcing future aggressive tightening which is negative for equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Taylor Rule framework implies policy is too accommodative.
failure_condition: The specified joint condition does not predict negative returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a walk-forward test over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-growth environment following a major economic shock, positive surprises in industrial production (INDPRO) have an outsized positive impact on investor sentiment and earnings forecasts, driving short-term equity gains.
evidence_refs: fact:Global economy is in a fragile recovery post-2008 GFC and 2011 Eurozone crisis.
failure_condition: The information coefficient of the 1-month change in INDPRO vs 5-day forward returns is not positive and significant during periods where GDP growth is below its long-term trend.

mechanism_narrative: Strong growth in real retail sales (RSAFS adjusted for CPIAUCSL) reflects robust consumer health, which directly supports revenues for a large portion of the S&P 500 and leads to positive returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is ~70% of US GDP.
failure_condition: The signal, defined as the 3-month change in RSAFS minus the 3-month change in CPIAUCSL, fails to achieve a positive rank IC with 20-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned near the zero lower bound, it creates a highly accommodative environment that suppresses risk-free rates, encourages risk-taking, and provides a persistent tailwind for equities.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed has maintained ZIRP since Dec 2008.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward S&P 500 return during periods where FEDFUNDS < 0.25% is not statistically higher than periods where FEDFUNDS > 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in housing starts (HOUST) is a leading indicator of a slowdown in a key cyclical sector, negatively impacting related industries and broader economic growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is a highly interest-rate sensitive and forward-looking sector.
failure_condition: Triggered instances are not followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of cases during the next full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A slowdown in real GDP growth (GDPC1) below its long-term potential rate signals a loss of economic momentum, leading to downward revisions in corporate earnings forecasts and negative pressure on stock prices.
evidence_refs: fact:Equity risk premium tends to increase when growth falters.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return when YoY GDP growth is below 2% is not statistically lower than when it is above 2% over a 10-year period.
